grounding_note: Expansion batch: more property-character and program-status disclosures in landlord-tenant intakes.

scenarios.opening_query: My landlord entered my place without any notice while I was away. I feel completely violated.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Real Property > Tenant (Commercial)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my pottery studio unit — a commercial lease, and he moved my kiln and inventory to 'show the space' to a prospective tenant.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my pottery studio — commercial lease — and he moved my kiln and inventory to show the space to someone else.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the entered premises are a dwelling or business space
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my apartment — he let himself in and went through my bedroom closet, and the statute requires 24-hour notice.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my apartment — he went through my bedroom closet, and he's supposed to give 24-hour notice.
scenarios.vetting_note: Entry rules are statutory for dwellings and purely contractual for commercial space.

scenarios.opening_query: We got a rent increase notice that's more than double and we can't possibly pay it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Real Property > Mobile Home (Tenant/Owner)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the space rent at our manufactured-home community — we own the home, and the state has special caps and notice rules for park space rent.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the space rent at our manufactured-home park — we own the home, and parks have special caps and notice rules.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the increase is mobile-home park space rent
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's our regular apartment — new owners bought the building and doubled everyone.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's our apartment — new building owners doubled everyone's rent.
scenarios.vetting_note: Space-rent statutes differ sharply from ordinary rent-increase law; row 374's park context.

scenarios.opening_query: The city condemned part of the building where I rent and I have nowhere to go on this timeline.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Real Property > Tenant (Commercial)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I rent the ground-floor bakery there — losing the space mid-holiday season kills my business, and my lease has a condemnation clause I don't understand.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I rent the ground-floor bakery — losing it mid-season kills my business, and my lease has a condemnation clause I can't parse.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the condemned space is a home or business
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my apartment on the third floor — me and my kids have 20 days under the notice.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's our third-floor apartment — my kids and I have 20 days.
scenarios.vetting_note: Condemnation displacement rights differ completely by property character.

scenarios.opening_query: My landlord is keeping my deposit and charging me for repairs on top, months after I moved out.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's $1,650 total and I've decided to take him to small claims myself — I need coaching on the deposit statute and evidence, not representation.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's $1,650 and I'm doing small claims myself — I just need coaching on the deposit statute and my evidence.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's also sent the made-up charges to collections and it's now on my credit — I want a lawyer on the whole thing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He sent the fake charges to collections and it hit my credit — I want a lawyer handling all of it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Deposit disputes at coaching scale vs credit-damage scale.

scenarios.opening_query: Our building's new management company is pushing out longtime tenants one by one and we're next.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || General Litigation > Civil Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: Every family they've pushed out is Latino — the white tenants with identical lease histories all got renewals, and three of us have kept the paperwork proving it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Every family pushed out is Latino — white tenants with identical histories got renewals, and we have the paperwork showing it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the displacement pattern tracks a protected class
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's not discriminatory — they're clearing everyone for a remodel; my fight is my own lease's renewal clause.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're clearing everyone for a remodel — my fight is my own renewal clause.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fair-housing pattern evidence reroutes an eviction defense into civil rights.

scenarios.opening_query: I rent a room in a house and the owner changed the locks on me after an argument.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'owner' is my ex-boyfriend — I moved into his house when we dated and stayed on after; there was never any rent agreement, just shared bills.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The owner is my ex — I moved in when we dated and stayed after. No rent agreement, just shared bills.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the occupancy is a tenancy or a former-partner living arrangement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's just a landlord I found online — I pay $700 by app on the first of every month, eight months running.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's just a landlord from a listing — $700 by app on the first, eight months straight.
scenarios.vetting_note: Roommate/ex-partner lockouts straddle tenancy and family law; the relationship+rent facts route.

scenarios.opening_query: My apartment has been without heat for two weeks and management just keeps 'escalating my ticket.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Real Property > ADA Issues/Animals/Accommodations
scenarios.hidden_fact: I have MS and heat regulation is medical for me — my neurologist wrote a letter asking them to prioritize my unit as a disability accommodation, and they've ignored it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I have MS — heat is medical for me. My neurologist's accommodation letter asked them to prioritize my unit and they ignored it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disability accommodation request has been denied or ignored
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No disability involved — it's a straight habitability failure affecting the whole building.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No disability issue — it's plain habitability, and it's the whole building.
scenarios.vetting_note: The ignored accommodation letter converts habitability into the LLTN-ADA route.

scenarios.opening_query: My tenant stopped paying and I found out they're running a business out of the unit too.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Landlord Residential || Real Property > Landlord Commercial
scenarios.hidden_fact: It IS a commercial unit — my retail storefront downtown; the 'business' is fine, the nonpayment is the issue, and commercial eviction rules apply.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my commercial storefront downtown — the business is fine, the nonpayment isn't, and commercial rules apply.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the leased unit is commercial or a dwelling
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a residential duplex unit — the unauthorized business is on top of the unpaid rent.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my residential duplex — the business is unauthorized on top of unpaid rent.
scenarios.vetting_note: Landlord role open; unit character hidden — commercial evictions skip residential protections.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm being evicted over a complaint I made and I know that's not supposed to be allowed.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The complaint was to the city code office, and their inspector confirmed violations — the city has a retaliation-protection program and I need it invoked alongside my defense.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My complaint was to the city code office and their inspector confirmed violations — the city has a retaliation-protection program I need invoked.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The complaint was just to the landlord himself about the mold — straight retaliatory-eviction defense.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The complaint went to the landlord about the mold — it's a straight retaliation defense.
scenarios.vetting_note: Municipal-program involvement adds an administrative route beside the eviction defense.

scenarios.opening_query: The place I rent was sold and the new owner says all the old agreements mean nothing now.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Real Property > Mobile Home (Tenant/Owner)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a mobile home park sale — we own our homes, and the state gives park residents special purchase and relocation rights when a park sells.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's our mobile home park that sold — we own the homes, and residents get special purchase and relocation rights in a park sale.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a mobile-home-park sale triggers resident statutory rights
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's an apartment building sale — my lease survives the sale and the new owner has to honor it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's an apartment building — my lease survives the sale.
scenarios.vetting_note: Park-sale statutes are a distinctive regime; 'the place I rent' hides it.
